sentence_reviews.text: Parameters are chosen to assign high probability to observed targets or data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probability-modeling and maximum-likelihood treatment; book landing page identifies the source as a probabilistic ML textbook, lines 21-29 and endorsements lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Maximum likelihood is exactly the procedure of choosing parameters that make observed data probable.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Information theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Linear models & generalized linear models, Hidden Markov models & CRFs, Matrix factorization & topic models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
